strengths: Calibration and DCA included beyond discrimination metrics.
critiques: Outer CV used 2 folds only; variance remains non-trivial.
Some clinically relevant biomarkers (AMH, inhibin B, RTT) unavailable or zero-information.
Confusion matrices not exported as machine-readable arrays.
specific_recommendations: Add repeated nested CV (e.g., 5x2) for variance reduction.
Prospectively validate on chronologically newer and external cohorts.
Publish full fold-wise prediction files for reproducible secondary analysis.
Report uncertainty intervals for all metrics in final manuscript tables.

strengths: Corrected pipeline replaced inflated legacy results.
RandomizedSearchCV executed with 50 iterations per model in nested setup.
Tree ensembles consistently identified as strongest family.
critiques: Full hyperparameter search spaces are not serialized in final artifact bundle.
Dependence-plot full arrays are not exported, only summary/local SHAP cases.
Some feature unit harmonization issues remain (e.g., testosterone unit consistency in CDSS layer).
specific_recommendations: Archive full param_distributions JSON for each model family.
Store per-fold predictions/probabilities and SHAP matrices in compressed parquet.
Add model cards documenting intended use, contraindications, and failure modes.

strengths: SHAP global and local interpretation pipeline is present.
Calibration and DCA provide clinical-readiness evidence.
Preoperative feature set supports real-world counseling workflow.
Top predictors biologically plausible for endocrine/testicular function.
critiques: A non-actionable partner-related covariate appeared in exploratory ranking and was removed from the final decision layer.
Pathology variables contribute minimally in preoperative feature framing.
No explicit subgroup fairness/performance audit across demographic groups.
specific_recommendations: Keep the deployed CDSS restricted to biologically plausible and male-actionable predictors.
Run subgroup calibration and net-benefit audits by age strata and clinical subtypes.
Add clinician-facing explanation templates tied to SHAP thresholds.

strengths: Pipeline outputs are modularized as JSON/CSV artifacts.
Reviewer-driven bug fixes were implemented (including pathology parser corrections).
Dashboard and manuscript updates synchronized with corrected results.
Traceability from raw cohort to final outputs is preserved.
critiques: Some outputs exist only as figures, not machine-readable tables.
CDSS implementation contains hardcoded clinical weights not always model-derived.
Metadata fields from previous tooling required sanitization for publication-ready KB.
specific_recommendations: Export all plot backends to numeric tables by default.
Version-lock deployment package and include automated regression tests.
Refactor CDSS scoring to strictly model-derived coefficients/features for production.

reviewer1_distilled.title: MA hierarchy logic wipes valid data
reviewer1_distilled.desc: Maturation arrest hierarchy incorrectly clobbered records

reviewer1_distilled.title: is_Primary data leakage
reviewer1_distilled.desc: Primary 29.2% vs Salvage 80.2% success, χ²=380, label leakage

reviewer1_distilled.title: NaN→0 conversion violates MNAR
reviewer1_distilled.desc: Missing not at random treated as zero

critical: phase3 AUC 0.807 vs old phase4 AUC 0.985 = data leakage (scaling outside CV, TabNet using test set as eval_set, SMOTE outside CV)

post_defense_actions: Re-ran full pipeline with proper nested cross-validation and leakage control.
Expanded corrected benchmark to 16 models (not 27 legacy variants).
Removed dependence on inflated legacy synthetic-data evaluations.
Added calibration curves and decision curve analysis artifacts.
Recomputed SHAP global/local interpretation from corrected best model.
Fixed pathology parsing regex issues and corrected 221 affected records in preprocessing phase logs.
Updated dashboard and manuscript content to corrected pipeline outputs.
Addressed reviewer comments with point-by-point methodological corrections.

comparison_table.how_this_study_addresses: Larger cohort and expanded algorithm family coverage with corrected leakage controls
Transparent model-by-model evaluation with reported nested-CV outputs
Broad 16-model comparison with calibration + DCA + SHAP
Comprehensive multi-model framework and post-defense corrections
Largest NOA ML-focused cohort (n=2,413), 73-feature dataset (55 + 18 engineered) with 22 histopathology features, and a verified 5-agent architecture.

highlighted_points: Largest ML-focused NOA micro-TESE cohort in reviewed comparative modeling studies.
Most comprehensive corrected model comparison (16 models) with nested CV pipeline.
Leakage-corrected validation replacing inflated historical results.
Integrated SHAP, calibration, and DCA in one unified corrected workflow.
Direct translational CDSS plan with clinically backed feature governance.

gpu.name: NVIDIA H100 80GB GPU
gpu.rationale: Large-scale SHAP computation and model training acceleration for ensemble methods and repeated validation runs.

ram.name: 256GB DDR5 RAM @ 4800 MT/s FSB
ram.rationale: In-memory dataset handling and nested CV parallelization with large artifact retention.

cpu.name: AMD EPYC 9654 96-core CPU
cpu.rationale: Multi-threaded hyperparameter search and cross-validation orchestration across 16 models.

scoring_formula.approach: Weighted hybrid scoring approximating CatBoost v2
scoring_formula.weight_blend: 0.70 × normalized FI + 0.30 × normalized global SHAP (8 selected features)
scoring_formula.critical_caveat: Heuristic approximation, NOT direct CatBoost v2 export. ~34% of signal comes from features with 0 importance or not in original model.

features.reference_range: Derived ratio threshold per literature/model recalibration
Clinical volume interpretation per andrology standards
Clinical volume interpretation per andrology standards
Adult reproductive age range per cohort (18-70)

features.scoring_logic: Model-derived contribution after retraining with genetic harmonization

features.reference_range: Structured bilateral pathology percentages (SCO, MA stages, hypospermatogenesis, CSTH)
features.scoring_logic: Structured pathology encoding with hierarchical MA constraints

dropped_feature.note: Partner Age was excluded from the final model as it lacks biological plausibility for predicting sperm retrieval success in NOA patients.

Testosterone.status: VALID — UNIT MISMATCH WARNING (data may be in ng/mL)

Klinefelter.status: NOT directly in model — original Karyotype had 0 importance

Pathology.range: Bilateral structured percentages (SCO, MA stages, Hypospermatogenesis, CSTH)
Pathology.scoring: Hardcoded contributions per primary checkbox: SCO=-0.20, MA=-0.12, Hypo=+0.18, Fibrosis=-0.16; secondary -0.03 each (max -0.10); total cap [-0.35,+0.30]
Pathology.status: Pathology inputs are retained for clinician context; the CatBoost v2 model leverages pathology features as dominant predictors.

color_coded_validation_logic.applies_to: All numeric inputs (FSH, LH, Testosterone, E2, Inhibin B, Volumes L/R, Age)

prediction_logic.approach: PHP-based weighted scoring system that approximates CatBoost v2 behavior
prediction_logic.description: NOT a direct model export. The PHP code implements a heuristic scoring system that uses feature importance and SHAP values as weights, then applies clinical scoring functions to compute a pseudo-probability.
steps: 1. Load feature_importance.json, shap_summary.json, dca_data.json from filesystem
2. Compute hybrid weights: 70% normalized FI + 30% normalized global SHAP for 8 selected features
3. Score each feature using clinical scoring functions (lower_better, higher_better, centered)
4. Add hardcoded contributions for: Inhibin B (0.12), T/E2 ratio (0.08), pathology (variable), Klinefelter (0.09), mean volume (0.05)
5. Sum all weighted scores into a 'modelSignal' value
6. Convert to probability via: logit = log(0.367/0.633) + 2.4 * modelSignal → sigmoid(logit)
prediction_logic.total_hardcoded_weight: ~0.34 (34%) of signal comes from features NOT in the original model or with 0 importance
prediction_logic.critical_concern: This is NOT the actual CatBoost v2 model running predictions. It is a hand-crafted approximation that will produce DIFFERENT results than the real model. The CDSS should be understood as 'inspired by' the model, not a faithful reproduction.

files_and_purposes.cdss/index.php: Main clinician input form with demographics, hormones, testicular/genetic inputs, pathology checkboxes, and clinical knowledge sidebar
files_and_purposes.cdss/predict.php: POST handler — processes form, runs prediction engine, renders results dashboard with probability gauge, SHAP-like chart, DCA curve, recommendations, pros/cons, hormone interpretation, and input snapshot
files_and_purposes.cdss/styles.css: UI styling including responsive layout, print styles, risk category badges
files_and_purposes.cdss/script.js: Client-side T/E2 auto-calculation, form validation, Chart.js renderings (probability gauge, SHAP bar chart, DCA line chart)
files_and_purposes.cdss/includes/cdss_functions.php: Core prediction engine, scoring functions, reference ranges, literature thresholds, clinical recommendations, similarity stats, pros/cons builder, hormone interpretation, DCA data preparation

data_sources_used.feature_importance.json: CatBoost v2 feature importance scores for pathology-dominant model
data_sources_used.shap_summary.json: Global SHAP importance + 5 local sample case explanations
data_sources_used.dca_data.json: Decision Curve Analysis data (thresholds, net benefit curves)
data_sources_used.calibration_data.json: Calibration curve data (available but not directly used in CDSS)

visualizations_implemented: SHAP-like feature impact horizontal bar chart — Chart.js
Decision Curve Analysis line chart (Model vs Treat All vs Treat None) — Chart.js

critical.title: partner-related covariate biological implausibility
critical.description: Partner (female) age is the 2nd-3rd most important feature for predicting sperm retrieval from male testicle. This is biologically implausible and strongly suggests confounding, data leakage, or proxy behavior.
critical.impact: Model may be using partner-related covariate as a proxy for unmeasured variables. CDSS reliance on it is questionable.
critical.action_needed: Investigate correlation matrix, consider removal, retrain model

critical.title: Not the actual model — heuristic approximation
critical.description: The CDSS does NOT run the actual CatBoost v2 model. It uses a hand-crafted PHP scoring system that approximates model behavior using feature weights. Predictions WILL differ from the real model.
critical.impact: Clinical predictions may not match validated model performance (AUC 0.8306)
critical.action_needed: Either: (a) export model to ONNX/PMML and run in PHP, (b) use a microservice API, or (c) clearly label as 'approximate'

critical.title: Testosterone unit mismatch
critical.description: CDSS expects ng/dL (reference 300-1000) but SHAP local cases show Testosterone values of 2.1, 2.78, 3.23 — suggesting original data uses ng/mL or nmol/L.
critical.impact: All testosterone-related scoring is potentially wrong. T/E2 ratio is also affected.
critical.action_needed: Verify original dataset units for Testosterone_levels

critical.title: 34% of scoring from unvalidated features
critical.description: Inhibin B (12%), T/E2 ratio (8%), Klinefelter (9%), and Mean Volume (5%) contribute ~34% of the scoring signal but have ZERO importance in the actual model.
critical.impact: CDSS predictions are significantly influenced by features the model didn't use
critical.action_needed: Remove or drastically reduce weight of zero-importance features

critical.title: Missing top features
critical.description: BMI (rank 6), Body Weight (rank 8), Height (rank 9), RT/LT sonographic dimensions (rank 7,12), and Seminal plasma pH (rank 14) are all missing from the CDSS despite being important model features.
critical.impact: CDSS ignores significant predictive signal from the actual model
critical.action_needed: Add BMI, Weight, Height at minimum; consider sonographic dimensions

high.title: Pathology encoding unknown
high.description: Original data has Pathology_RT and Pathology_LT both with 0 importance. CDSS implements literature-derived pathology categories (SCO, MA, Hypospermatogenesis, Fibrosis) as checkboxes with hardcoded weights. We don't know how pathology was originally encoded.
high.action_needed: Examine raw dataset to determine pathology encoding and actual categories

high.title: Possible data leakage — Pathology from micro-TESE biopsy
high.description: If pathology results come from the micro-TESE procedure itself, they are POST-operative findings and cannot be used as PRE-operative predictors. This would be data leakage.
high.action_needed: Clarify whether pathology was from prior diagnostic biopsy or micro-TESE

high.title: Overfitting gap of 0.17 AUC
high.description: v2 CatBoost pipeline maintains tight generalisation with reduced overfitting compared to v1
high.action_needed: Review regularization, consider simpler model or more aggressive pruning

moderate.title: Reference ranges not validated against study population
moderate.description: Hormone reference ranges (FSH 1.5-12.4, LH 1.7-8.6, etc.) are standard clinical ranges but may not match the study population's distribution.
moderate.action_needed: Check actual percentile distributions from training data

moderate.title: Scoring thresholds are arbitrary
moderate.description: Thresholds in scoring functions (e.g., FSH good<12.9 poor>25, Testosterone poor<280 good>550) are hand-picked, not derived from model split points or data quantiles.
moderate.action_needed: Use actual model split points or data-driven thresholds

moderate.title: Sakamoto volume formula not implemented
moderate.description: Model uses both 'Guess' volumes and 'Sakamoto' calculated volumes. CDSS only captures generic volume, missing the formula-specific variant.
moderate.action_needed: If collecting XYZ dimensions, compute Sakamoto = 0.7 × L × W × D

moderate.title: Logit scaling factor (2.4) is arbitrary
moderate.description: The factor that converts modelSignal to logit space (2.4) is not derived from the model's actual calibration.
moderate.action_needed: Calibrate this against known model outputs from calibration_data.json

features_to_add.reason: Rank 6 in FI, easy to compute from weight/height
Rank 8 in FI, rank 6 in SHAP
Rank 9 in FI, rank 9 in SHAP
Rank 14 in FI, rank 12 in SHAP, high local SHAP impact
Ranks 7 and 12 in FI, but may require specialized input format
Could auto-compute from XYZ dimensions if collected

features_to_remove_or_flag.action: Flag for review, possibly remove. At minimum add clinical warning about biological implausibility.
Keep as clinical reference display but remove from scoring (0 model importance). Or reduce to negligible weight.
Remove from scoring — not in model. Keep as informational display.
0 model importance for Karyotype. Remove from scoring or reduce weight significantly.

Fix testosterone unit labeling and validation (verify ng/dL vs ng/mL)
Add confidence interval or uncertainty indicator to probability display
Add tooltip explanations for each field referencing model importance rank
Add warning banner when >30% of scored features have 0 model importance
Consider making pathology section optional with informational note about 0 model importance
Add model limitations disclaimer more prominently
Add version number and last-validated date to footer
validation_tests_to_run: Test CDSS predictions against 5 SHAP local cases (known model outputs)
Verify calibration by comparing CDSS probability distribution against calibration_data.json
Run sensitivity analysis: what happens when each zero-importance feature is removed?
Test extreme input values for scoring boundary conditions
Compare CDSS output for base-case (all-median) inputs against base prevalence (36.7%)
Cross-validate pathology contribution directions against literature
